title: IdeaSpec
description: Structured idea specification produced by I1 Socratic Interviewer. Schema introduced in semver v1.6.0 (3-batch × 3–4 Qs, 10–12 fields). Phase 9 (B-1, shipped v1.10.0) trimmed required to 4 fields (persona / platform / killer_feature / constraint) with 5–8 optional; B-3 added 'Skip interview' which writes only the 3 required keys (`idea_summary`, `_filled_ratio`, `_schema_version`). Soft anchor — every semantic field may be 'unknown' or null so 26 advocates can still reframe creatively while the filled subset anchors Rule 9 idea-drift containment. The filled subset is summarized by `_filled_ratio` (see field doc) and routed by A-4 tiered fallback in `agents/ideation/ideation-lead.md` §1. Note: 'v1.6 / v1.7 audit umbrellas' are feature-track names; the corresponding semver release tags are listed in CHANGELOG.md.

idea_summary.description: Original one-liner from /pf:new, verbatim. S-3 cap (#65, v1.7.0+ hardening): bounded to 5000 chars so a poisoned idea.spec.json cannot blow up advocate prompt size / Rule 9 containment budget.

profile.description: Concrete user — e.g. 'solo founder under time pressure', not 'small business'.

primary_pain.description: One-sentence description of the user's current pain point.

usage_frequency.description: Canonical values: daily | weekly | monthly | episodic | unknown. Free-form text (e.g. 'twice a day') also allowed so the AskUserQuestion 'Other' path is never silently discarded.

platform.description: Canonical values: web | mobile | desktop | api | hybrid | unknown. Free-form text (e.g. 'Slack bot', 'Telegram') is preserved verbatim so Batch B 'Other' answers are never coerced into a lossy bucket.

sync_model.description: Canonical values: real-time | eventual | batch | unknown. Free-form text allowed.

functional.description: What practical outcome does the user achieve?

emotional.description: How does the user want to feel — calm, confident, in control?

social.description: How does the user want to be perceived by others?

must_have_constraints.description: Hard constraints captured by I1 Batch C. Canonical `type` buckets: regulatory | budget | latency | team_size | platform | data_residency | other. Free-form strings (e.g. 'air-gapped deployment', 'mandated vendor X') belong under `type: "other"` with the verbatim text in `value` — so the Batch C 'Other' path is never silently dropped. S-3 (v1.7.0+): array is capped at 20 entries with per-field maxLength so a poisoned idea.spec.json cannot blow up advocate prompt size / Rule 9 containment budget. T-3 (v1.7 audit): the canonical 7-bucket `type.enum` and the `scripts/normalize-constraints.py` reference normalizer keep the cache key stable across I1 implementations.

killer_feature.description: The 1 thing that differentiates this from existing solutions. 'unknown' or null if Socratic phase did not pin it down.

non_goals.description: Explicitly out-of-scope items. Empty array allowed (soft anchor). S-3 (v1.7.0+): array is capped at 20 entries, each item ≤500 chars, to bound prompt expansion.

monetization_model.description: Canonical values: freemium | subscription | one-time | b2b_saas | open_source | ads | transaction_fee | unknown. Free-form text allowed for Batch C 'Other' answers (e.g. 'sponsorship', 'grant-funded').

success_metric.description: How would you measure if this succeeded — revenue, DAU, retention, task completion time, NPS?

_filled_ratio.description: Fraction of 9 semantic slots populated with a non-null / non-'unknown' value. Tiered fallback in `agents/ideation/ideation-lead.md` §1 (Phase 9 A-4, shipped v1.10.0) reads this. Counting rule (deterministic across I1 implementations): • Slot 1 — `idea_summary` (always filled, `minLength: 1` is required). • Slots 2–4 — `target_persona`, `primary_surface`, `jobs_to_be_done` count as filled IFF at least one sub-field is non-null/non-unknown (binary, NOT proportional to sub-field count). • Slots 5–7 — `killer_feature`, `monetization_model`, `success_metric` each count as 1 slot, filled IFF non-null AND non-'unknown'. • Slots 8–9 — `must_have_constraints`, `non_goals` arrays count as filled IFF `length >= 1` (empty array = 'user did not answer', NOT an affirmative 'no constraints'). Denominator is 9. Boundary cases worth knowing: B-1 fast path (4 required answered, all optionals skipped) yields 5/9 ≈ 0.56 (medium tier under A-4); B-3 'Skip interview' yields 1/9 ≈ 0.11 (fallback tier — spec dropped from advocate prompt). No path is hard-blocked; A-4 only adjusts how strongly advocates use the spec.

_schema_version.description: Schema version for forward-compat. v1.5.x runs have no idea.spec.json; v1.6.x introduced this schema.
